summary: Funk‑laden Shakedown Street & soaring Scarlet>Fire energize Berlin show
blurb: The set bursts open with Let The Good Times Roll and rides a funk‑filled Shakedown Street before launching into a bright Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain. Jerry’s guitar shines while the dual keyboards add sparkle, and the true‑stereo drum mix gives the performance extra drive. Highlights include Brown Eyed Women, Terrapin Station and a solid Deal.
review:
October 19, 1990 at Berlin’s Internationales Congress Centrum captured one of the Dead’s most kinetic European outings. The first set kicked off with a rollicking Let The Good Times Roll and quickly settled into a groove‑heavy Shakedown Street, where Jerry’s lead lines were funk‑charged and Vince Welnick’s organ (with Bruce Hornsby’s piano) cut through brilliantly. Little Red Rooster and Brown Eyed Women kept the momentum rolling, leading to an exuberant Deal that closed the set on a high note.

The second half delivered the classic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain pairing, soaring with Jerry’s lyrical phrasing despite a few lyric slips. Terrapin Station unfolded with its characteristic grandeur, while the rhythm section—Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann—benefited from an outstanding true‑stereo drum mix that added depth and swing. Phil Lesh’s growly Aural‑Exciter bass anchored the jams, and the dual keyboards provided bright textures throughout. The show rounded out with a heartfelt encore of It’s All Over Now Baby Blue, leaving the intimate ICC crowd buzzing.

Among the available sources, the matrix recording (gd90-10-19.dsbd-matrix.dieter.12892.sbeok.shnf) stands out for its crystal‑clear soundboard clarity, excellent stereo separation, and low noise floor, making it the definitive way to experience this high‑energy performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, one mixed and one negative; the overall tone leans positive but includes some criticism (mixed remarks about off parts). This yields a moderate +1.0 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent matrix source with superb stereo drum mix, crisp instrument separation and captures the band’s high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Funk‑laden Shakedown Street showcases Jerry’s lead and tight keyboard interplay.
Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain delivers a soaring, melodic peak.
True‑stereo drum mix gives Mickey and Bill an extra dimension of drive.
Energetic set pacing with strong first‑set groove and powerful second‑set jams.

band_performance.Jerry: Shows occasional lyric slip‑ups but his lead on Shakedown Street is funk‑filled and he drives the Scarlet>Fire sequence with characteristic phrasing.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass is thick and growly, reflecting the Aural‑Exciter phase, and stays solid through the extended jams.
band_performance.Brent: Vince Welnick’s organ shines on Shakedown Street, while Bruce Hornsby adds bright piano textures; together they complement the band despite occasional clash noted by some listeners.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is captured in true‑stereo depth, propelling the set and adding extra swing to the funk sections.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann locks in with Hart, delivering a tight, driving groove that underpins the high‑energy performance.
